symptoms.symptom_name: Six months of progressive lower urinary tract symptoms (hesitancy, weak stream, nocturia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Obstructive LUTS in a 74-year-old man raises prostate pathology, but is non-specific between BPH and carcinoma.
LUTS is more commonly benign — the anchoring trap; malignancy is distinguished by the systemic and neurological features, not the LUTS alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Three months of deep unremitting lower back and pelvic bone pain, unresponsive to simple analgesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive, analgesia-resistant axial bone pain in an older man is a classic feature of skeletal metastatic disease.
Constitutional red flag raising underlying malignancy; non-specific to site.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe escalation of chronic back pain over ~2 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden worsening of metastatic back pain can mark vertebral collapse and new epidural compression.

symptoms.symptom_name: New bilateral lower-limb weakness with collapse on standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral motor loss is a cardinal feature of cord compression and the trigger for emergency action.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute lumbar disc herniation with radiculopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A single disc/root lesion does not usually produce symmetrical bilateral weakness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral leg numbness reported by patient

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral sensory disturbance with weakness points to a cord-level lesion; the precise sensory level requires examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Erectile dysfunction over the past year

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Common and multifactorial in older men; weakly consistent with local prostate/pelvic disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, irregular, asymmetrical nodular prostate on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard, irregular, nodular prostate is highly suspicious for carcinoma rather than BPH; obtainable only by clinician DRE — sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper-motor-neuron signs — symmetrically reduced lower-limb power (3/5), brisk knee/ankle reflexes (3+), bilateral extensor plantar (Babinski) responses

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Spinal cord (central) lesion above the conus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: UMN pattern localises the lesion to the cord rather than a peripheral root; requires a neurological examination the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sensory level to pinprick at approximately T10 (below umbilicus)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A defined sensory level localises the compression; established only by examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved bladder/bowel continence, no saddle anaesthesia, intact anal sphincter tone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved sphincter and saddle sensation makes conus/cauda involvement less likely, but does NOT reduce the emergency of cord compression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised spinal percussion tenderness over the lower thoracic/lumbar spine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal percussion tenderness suggests a structural (metastatic) vertebral lesion at that level.
Absence of fever (with no IV-drug-use history) makes infective epidural compression less likely, though not excluded.

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated, consistent with metastatic prostate carcinoma
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot be obtained via telehealth; part of the ED/urology work-up, not an AI Doctor deliverable.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Supports the diagnosis but cannot be obtained remotely; never available to the AI in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: Whole-spine MRI showing epidural cord compression by metastatic deposit

investigation_details.expected_result: Epidural metastatic deposit compressing the thoracolumbar cord with a defined level
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Definitive imaging that confirms and localises MSCC; only available in hospital — the AI escalates to obtain it, it cannot order or interpret it remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Whole-spine MRI is the definitive test to confirm/exclude and localise cord compression.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe localised back pain + progressive bilateral leg weakness + a sensory level is the classic cord-compression triad and a neurosurgical emergency.
LUTS + analgesia-resistant bone pain + weight loss + a hard nodular prostate + elevated PSA together identify metastatic prostate cancer as the substrate for the cord compression.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive test to confirm and localise malignant cord compression; must be arranged emergently via ED — the AI escalates rather than orders it.
Supports metastatic prostate cancer as the underlying cause; part of the hospital/urology work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Digital rectal examination by a clinician
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies the hard nodular prostate; an in-person examination, not a telehealth action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Isolated outpatient lumbar spine X-ray as the primary investigation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Inadequate and dangerously slow for suspected cord compression — MRI and emergency review are required; a plain film must not be used to 'reassure' or defer.
